title: Best Password Manager for iPhone and iOS (2026): Top 5 Compared
excerpt: iOS 18 ships with Apple Passwords, but it has limitations that make third-party managers worth considering. Here's how NordPass, Bitwarden, 1Password, Apple Passwords, and Dashlane compare for iPhone users in 2026.
content:
iOS 18 and the Built-In Passwords App

Apple's iOS 18 ships with a standalone Passwords app — a significant upgrade from the hidden Settings → Passwords menu. It supports passwords, passkeys, verification codes (TOTP), Wi-Fi passwords, and shared group vaults. For people who live entirely in the Apple ecosystem, it's now genuinely usable. But it has hard limits: no cross-platform support for Android or Windows, no Travel Mode, no advanced breach monitoring, and it can't be used as a standalone app on non-Apple devices.

If those limitations matter to you — and for most people at least one does — here's how the third-party options compare.

Top 5 iPhone Password Managers Compared

AppFace IDSafari autofillApple WatchPrice/yr
1Password✅✅✅$36
NordPass✅✅❌$24
Bitwarden✅✅❌Free / $10
Apple Passwords✅✅ (best)✅Free
Dashlane✅✅❌$33

1Password — Best Overall for iPhone

1Password has the most polished iOS app of any third-party manager. Face ID locks the vault, Safari autofill works seamlessly across all apps, and the Apple Watch app lets you view passwords on your wrist when your phone isn't handy. The iOS widget shows recently used items without unlocking the full vault. Travel Mode — which hides selected vaults when crossing international borders — is a unique feature unavailable elsewhere.

The Shortcut integration lets you build automations that pull from 1Password, which is useful for power users who automate parts of their workflow.

NordPass — Best Combination of Price and Features

NordPass at $24/year offers Face ID, Safari autofill, and a clean iPhone UI at about two-thirds the price of 1Password. It uses XChaCha20 encryption rather than AES-256 — a modern algorithm with slightly better performance on mobile hardware. The main gap is no Apple Watch app and less deep iOS integration compared to 1Password.

Bitwarden — Best Free Option

Bitwarden's free tier works on iPhone with Face ID and Safari autofill — you get full cross-device sync for zero cost. The iOS app is functional, though less refined than 1Password's. Premium ($10/year) adds built-in TOTP codes. For budget-conscious users who also use Android or Windows, Bitwarden is the clear choice: it's the only full-featured cross-platform manager that's genuinely free.

Apple Passwords — Best for Apple-Only Users

If every device you own is an Apple device, the built-in Passwords app in iOS 18 is worth a serious look. It has the deepest Safari integration of any option (passwords fill before you even tap the field), passkey support, TOTP codes, and iCloud Keychain backup. Shared vaults work seamlessly with family members via iCloud Family Sharing. The tradeoff: no Android app, limited Windows support (only via iCloud for Windows), and no advanced admin features.

Migrating from iCloud Keychain

If you're currently using iCloud Keychain and want to switch to a third-party manager, the process is straightforward: go to Settings → Passwords, select all entries, and export to a CSV. Most password managers (1Password, Bitwarden, NordPass) import this format directly. After importing, verify a few logins work, then disable iCloud Keychain autofill in Settings → Passwords → Password Options.

Our Pick

For iPhone users who also use non-Apple devices: 1Password. For iPhone-only users on a budget: Bitwarden free tier. For Apple-only households who want zero friction: Apple Passwords.
